assumptions: Commercial reasoning is directional until user interviews, traffic, or payment intent are measured.
Architecture choices must stay aligned with the accepted creative direction.

directions.audience: a narrow first user group with an urgent, repeated workflow
directions.name: Focused MVP
directions.promise: Ship the smallest useful version of: CLI tool that converts markdown planning packets to HTML and PDF
directions.reason: fastest path to validation and lowest scope risk
directions.target_user_pain: The user needs one painful job completed reliably before broader platform value matters.

directions.audience: users who know the outcome they want but not the sequence of choices
directions.name: Guided Workflow
directions.promise: Turn the problem into a step-by-step decision journey.
directions.reason: best fit when the idea is vague and users need momentum more than customization
directions.target_user_pain: The user is blocked by ambiguity, not lack of tools.

directions.audience: teams that need shared confidence before implementation
directions.name: Evidence Dashboard
directions.promise: Make options, evidence, confidence, and next actions visible.
directions.reason: commercially strongest when stakeholders must compare options and justify tradeoffs
directions.target_user_pain: The team needs decisions to be inspectable, repeatable, and defensible.

creative_decision.goal: CLI tool that converts markdown planning packets to HTML and PDF
creative_decision.next_decision: Confirm the selected direction before architecture is treated as accepted.

selected_direction.audience: a narrow first user group with an urgent, repeated workflow

selected_direction.reason: fastest path to validation and lowest scope risk
selected_direction.target_user_pain: The user needs one painful job completed reliably before broader platform value matters.

evidence.summary: Package your Node.js project into an executable cli-demo
evidence.title: vercel/pkg

evidence.summary: Node.js has built-in support for command-line argument parsing and CLI tool development. cli-demo
evidence.title: Building CLI Tools with Node.js

project.goal: CLI tool that converts markdown planning packets to HTML and PDF

acceptance_criteria: Preserve the accepted ADR unless new evidence is recorded.
Run the relevant structured harness commands and explain remaining failures.
Keep implementation scope inside the accepted creative direction.
Return to Project Forge when direction, architecture, or harness assumptions become stale.
superpowers.assignment: Consume this packet and implement without re-litigating accepted product and architecture decisions.
consume_steps: Read this packet and the Markdown handoff.
Open the ADR, creative decision, inventory, and evidence only when more detail is needed.
Use Superpowers implementation workflows after accepting the packet.
superpowers.first_task: Implement the smallest user-visible workflow from the accepted creative direction and prove it with the harness contract.
guardrails: Project Forge does not replace TDD, debugging, code review, worktrees, or branch completion.
Do not silently replace provisional evidence with unsupported claims.
Do not execute commands that are absent from the harness contract.
